title: oldenlandia humifusa
intro: Oldenlandia humifusa, also known as creeping foxglove, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine in East Asia, particularly in China and Korea, for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, Oldenlandia humifusa is often combined with other herbs to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that are consumed to promote overall health and well-being, particularly for its potential to boost the immune system and improve respiratory function.
